comment_text.sn22.81:1.3: This story is expanded at Ud 4.5, where the Buddha reflects on how crowded he is with people all around. But the full story is told in the Vinaya at Kd 10. The local Saṅgha at Kosambī had fallen into dispute over a trivial matter. They would not settle it even at the Buddha’s urging, so he left.
comment_text.sn22.81:3.2: Linguistically, pārileyyaka means “the place of the son of Parila”. However, the commentary tells at length of how an elephant living in this “protected forest grove” (Ud 4.5:2.4) attended on the Buddha with his friend, the monkey Madhuda, bringing the Buddha food and caring for his every need. The elephant was named Pārileyya (Ja 488:24.1), and the forest Pārileyyaka after him.
comment_text.sn22.81:4.4: Anantara is a synonym for akālika, sandiṭṭhika, and diṭṭheva dhamme, all of which mean “in the present life, without delay or interim”.
comment_text.sn22.81:10.7: The repeated demonstrative pronouns in the Pali so loko so attā (literally, “This is the cosmos, this is the self”) affirm an emphatic and absolute identity: “The cosmos and the self are one and the same”. As the central thesis of the Upaniṣads, it has a lexical parallel at Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 4.4.22, which argues for the renunciate life: “What can descendants do for those for whom the self and the cosmos are one and the same?” (ayamātmāyaṁ loka). Similarly, BU 1.2.7 says of the sacrificial fire as the sun, “these worlds are its self” (tasyeme lokā ātmānaḥ), and BU 1.4.15 says one should “worship the cosmos as the self”(ātmānam eva lokam upāsīta). See also the last words of a father to his son at 1.5.17: “You are divinity, you are the sacrifice, you are the cosmos” (tvaṁ brahma tvaṁ yajñas tvaṁ loka).

root_text.sn22.81:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.sn22.81:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya kosambiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.sn22.81:1.3: Kosambiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto sāmaṁ senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya anāmantetvā upaṭṭhāke anapaloketvā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ eko adutiyo cārikaṁ pakkāmi.
root_text.sn22.81:2.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu acirapakkantassa bhagavato yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.81:2.2: “esāvuso ānanda, bhagavā sāmaṁ senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya anāmantetvā upaṭṭhāke anapaloketvā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ eko adutiyo cārikaṁ pakkanto”ti.
root_text.sn22.81:2.3: “Yasmiṁ, āvuso, samaye bhagavā sāmaṁ senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaramādāya anāmantetvā upaṭṭhāke anapaloketvā bhikkhusaṅghaṁ eko adutiyo cārikaṁ pakkamati, ekova bhagavā tasmiṁ samaye viharitukāmo hoti;
root_text.sn22.81:2.4: na bhagavā tasmiṁ samaye kenaci anubandhitabbo hotī”ti.
root_text.sn22.81:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā anupubbena cārikaṁ caramāno yena pālileyyakaṁ tadavasari.
root_text.sn22.81:3.2: Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā pālileyyake viharati bhaddasālamūle.
root_text.sn22.81:3.3: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.sn22.81:3.4: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn22.81:3.5: “cirassutā kho no, āvuso ānanda, bhagavato sammukhā dhammī kathā;
root_text.sn22.81:3.6: icchāma mayaṁ, āvuso ānanda, bhagavato sammukhā dhammiṁ kathaṁ sotun”ti.
root_text.sn22.81:4.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando tehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ yena pālileyyakaṁ bhaddasālamūlaṁ yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.sn22.81:4.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te bhikkhū bhagavā dhammiyā kathāya sandassesi samādapesi samuttejesi sampahaṁsesi.
root_text.sn22.81:4.3: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa bhikkhuno evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn22.81:4.4: “kathaṁ nu kho jānato kathaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hotī”ti?
root_text.sn22.81:4.5: Atha kho bhagavā tassa bhikkhuno cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn22.81:4.14: Evaṁ vicayaso desito, bhikkhave, mayā dhammo.
root_text.sn22.81:4.15: Evaṁ vicayaso desite kho, bhikkhave, mayā dhamme atha ca panidhekaccassa bhikkhuno evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn22.81:4.16: ‘kathaṁ nu kho jānato kathaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hotī’ti?
root_text.sn22.81:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, jānato kathaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti?
root_text.sn22.81:5.2: Idha bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano ariyānaṁ adassāvī ariyadhammassa akovido ariyadhamme avinīto, sappurisānaṁ adassāvī sappurisadhammassa akovido sappurisadhamme avinīto
root_text.sn22.81:5.4: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, samanupassanā saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:5.5: So pana saṅkhāro kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo?
root_text.sn22.81:5.6: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:5.8: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:5.10: Sāpi vedanā, sopi phasso anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:5.12: Evampi kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:6.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati;
root_text.sn22.81:6.2: api ca kho rūpavantaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.81:6.3: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, samanupassanā saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:6.4: So pana saṅkhāro kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo?
root_text.sn22.81:6.5: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:6.7: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:6.12: Evampi kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:7.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati;
root_text.sn22.81:7.2: api ca kho attani rūpaṁ samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.81:7.3: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, samanupassanā saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:7.4: So pana saṅkhāro kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo?
root_text.sn22.81:7.5: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:7.7: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:7.12: Evampi kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:8.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati, na attani rūpaṁ samanupassati;
root_text.sn22.81:8.2: api ca kho rūpasmiṁ attānaṁ samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.81:8.3: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, samanupassanā saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:8.4: So pana saṅkhāro kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo?
root_text.sn22.81:8.5: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:8.7: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:8.12: Evampi kho, bhikkhave, jānato …pe… āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:9.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na rūpavantaṁ attānaṁ, na attani rūpaṁ, na rūpasmiṁ attānaṁ samanupassati;
root_text.sn22.81:9.2: api ca kho vedanaṁ attato samanupassati, api ca kho vedanāvantaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati, api ca kho attani vedanaṁ samanupassati, api ca kho vedanāya attānaṁ samanupassati;
root_text.sn22.81:9.4: api ca kho saṅkhāre attato samanupassati, api ca kho saṅkhāravantaṁ attānaṁ samanupassati, api ca kho attani saṅkhāre samanupassati, api ca kho saṅkhāresu attānaṁ samanupassati;
root_text.sn22.81:9.5: api ca kho viññāṇaṁ attato samanupassati, api ca kho viññāṇavantaṁ attānaṁ, api ca kho attani viññāṇaṁ, api ca kho viññāṇasmiṁ attānaṁ samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.81:9.6: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, samanupassanā saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:9.8: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:9.10: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:9.15: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:10.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati,
root_text.sn22.81:10.7: ‘so attā so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti.
root_text.sn22.81:10.8: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, sassatadiṭṭhi saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:10.10: evampi kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:11.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati,
root_text.sn22.81:11.7: ‘so attā so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti.
root_text.sn22.81:11.9: ‘no cassaṁ no ca me siyā nābhavissaṁ na me bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn22.81:11.10: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, ucchedadiṭṭhi saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:11.11: So pana saṅkhāro kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo?
root_text.sn22.81:11.12: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:11.14: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco …pe…
root_text.sn22.81:11.15: evampi kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hoti.
root_text.sn22.81:12.1: Na heva kho rūpaṁ attato samanupassati, na vedanaṁ …
root_text.sn22.81:12.5: na viññāṇasmiṁ attato samanupassati, nāpi evaṁdiṭṭhi hoti:
root_text.sn22.81:12.6: ‘so attā so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti;
root_text.sn22.81:12.8: ‘no cassaṁ no ca me siyā nābhavissaṁ na me bhavissatī’ti;
root_text.sn22.81:12.9: api ca kho kaṅkhī hoti vicikicchī aniṭṭhaṅgato saddhamme.
root_text.sn22.81:12.10: Yā kho pana sā, bhikkhave, kaṅkhitā vicikicchitā aniṭṭhaṅgatatā saddhamme saṅkhāro so.
root_text.sn22.81:12.11: So pana saṅkhāro kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo?
root_text.sn22.81:12.12: Avijjāsamphassajena, bhikkhave, vedayitena phuṭṭhassa assutavato puthujjanassa uppannā taṇhā;
root_text.sn22.81:12.14: Iti kho, bhikkhave, sopi saṅkhāro anicco saṅkhato paṭiccasamuppanno.
root_text.sn22.81:12.19: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, jānato evaṁ passato anantarā āsavānaṁ khayo hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn22.81:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.sn22.81:1.2: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Kosambī for alms.
translation_text.sn22.81:1.3: After the meal, on his return from almsround, he set his lodgings in order himself. Taking his bowl and robe, without informing his attendants or taking leave of the mendicant Saṅgha, he set out to go wandering alone, with no companion.
translation_text.sn22.81:2.1: Then, not long after the Buddha had left, one of the mendicants went to Venerable Ānanda and told him what had happened.
translation_text.sn22.81:2.3: Ānanda said, “Reverend, when the Buddha leaves like this it means he wants to stay alone.
translation_text.sn22.81:2.4: At this time no-one should follow him.”
translation_text.sn22.81:3.1: Then the Buddha, traveling stage by stage, arrived at Pārileyyaka,
translation_text.sn22.81:3.2: where he stayed at the root of a sacred sal tree.
translation_text.sn22.81:3.3: Then several mendicants went up to Venerable Ānanda and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn22.81:3.4: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they sat down to one side and said to him,
translation_text.sn22.81:3.5: “Reverend, it’s been a long time since we’ve heard a Dhamma talk from the Buddha.
translation_text.sn22.81:3.6: We wish to hear a Dhamma talk from the Buddha.”
translation_text.sn22.81:4.1: Then Venerable Ānanda together with those mendicants went to Pārileyyaka to see the Buddha. They bowed and sat down to one side,
translation_text.sn22.81:4.2: and the Buddha educated, encouraged, fired up, and inspired them with a Dhamma talk.
translation_text.sn22.81:4.3: Now at that time one of the monks had the thought,
translation_text.sn22.81:4.4: “How do you know and see in order to end the defilements without delay?”
translation_text.sn22.81:4.5: Then the Buddha, knowing that monk’s train of thought, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn22.81:4.7: I’ve analytically taught the four kinds of mindfulness meditation,
translation_text.sn22.81:4.9: the four bases of psychic power,
translation_text.sn22.81:4.14: That’s how I’ve taught the Dhamma analytically.
translation_text.sn22.81:4.15: Though I’ve taught the Dhamma analytically, still a certain mendicant present here has this thought:
translation_text.sn22.81:4.16: ‘How do you know and see in order to end the defilements without delay?’
translation_text.sn22.81:5.1: And how, mendicants, do you know and see in order to end the defilements without delay?
translation_text.sn22.81:5.2: Take an unlearned ordinary person who has not seen the noble ones, and is neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the noble ones. They’ve not seen true persons, and are neither skilled nor trained in the teaching of the true persons.
translation_text.sn22.81:5.4: But that regarding is just a conditioned phenomenon.
translation_text.sn22.81:5.5: And what’s the source, origin, birthplace, and inception of that conditioned phenomenon?
translation_text.sn22.81:5.6: When an unlearned ordinary person is struck by feelings born of contact with ignorance, craving arises.
translation_text.sn22.81:5.7: That conditioned phenomenon is born from that.
translation_text.sn22.81:5.8: So that conditioned phenomenon is impermanent, conditioned, and dependently originated.
translation_text.sn22.81:5.11: and that ignorance are also impermanent, conditioned, and dependently originated.
translation_text.sn22.81:5.12: That’s how you should know and see in order to end the defilements without delay.
translation_text.sn22.81:6.1: Perhaps they don’t regard form as self,
translation_text.sn22.81:6.2: but they still regard self as possessing form.
translation_text.sn22.81:6.3: But that regarding is just a conditioned phenomenon. …
translation_text.sn22.81:7.1: Perhaps they don’t regard form as self, or self as possessing form,
translation_text.sn22.81:7.2: but they still regard form in self.
translation_text.sn22.81:7.3: But that regarding is just a conditioned phenomenon. …
translation_text.sn22.81:8.1: Perhaps they don’t regard form as self, or self as possessing form, or form in self,
translation_text.sn22.81:8.2: but they still regard self in form.
translation_text.sn22.81:8.3: But that regarding is just a conditioned phenomenon. …
translation_text.sn22.81:9.1: Perhaps they don’t regard form as self, or self as possessing form, or form in self, or self in form.
translation_text.sn22.81:9.2: But they regard feeling as self …
translation_text.sn22.81:9.6: But that regarding is just a conditioned phenomenon.
translation_text.sn22.81:9.7: And what’s the source of that conditioned phenomenon?
translation_text.sn22.81:9.8: When an unlearned ordinary person is struck by feelings born of contact with ignorance, craving arises.
translation_text.sn22.81:9.9: That conditioned phenomenon is born from that.
translation_text.sn22.81:9.10: So that conditioned phenomenon is impermanent, conditioned, and dependently originated.
translation_text.sn22.81:9.14: and that ignorance are also impermanent, conditioned, and dependently originated.
translation_text.sn22.81:9.15: That’s how you should know and see in order to end the defilements without delay.
translation_text.sn22.81:10.6: Still, they have such a view:
translation_text.sn22.81:10.7: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn22.81:10.8: But that eternalist view is just a conditioned phenomenon.
translation_text.sn22.81:10.9: And what’s the source of that conditioned phenomenon? …
translation_text.sn22.81:10.10: That’s how you should know and see in order to end the defilements without delay.
translation_text.sn22.81:11.6: Nor do they have such a view:
translation_text.sn22.81:11.7: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn22.81:11.8: Still, they have such a view:
translation_text.sn22.81:11.9: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.sn22.81:11.10: But that annihilationist view is just a conditioned phenomenon.
translation_text.sn22.81:11.11: And what’s the source of that conditioned phenomenon? …
translation_text.sn22.81:11.15: That’s how you should know and see in order to end the defilements without delay.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.1: Perhaps they don’t regard form or feeling
translation_text.sn22.81:12.5: Nor do they have such a view:
translation_text.sn22.81:12.6: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn22.81:12.7: Nor do they have such a view:
translation_text.sn22.81:12.8: ‘I might not be, and it might not be mine. I will not be, and it will not be mine.’
translation_text.sn22.81:12.9: Still, they have doubts and uncertainties. They’re undecided about the true teaching.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.10: That doubt and uncertainty, the indecision about the true teaching, is just a conditioned phenomenon.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.11: And what’s the source of that conditioned phenomenon?
translation_text.sn22.81:12.12: When an unlearned ordinary person is struck by feelings born of contact with ignorance, craving arises.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.13: That conditioned phenomenon is born from that.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.14: So that conditioned phenomenon is impermanent, conditioned, and dependently originated.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.18: and that ignorance are also impermanent, conditioned, and dependently originated.
translation_text.sn22.81:12.19: That’s how you should know and see in order to end the defilements without delay.”

reference_text.sn22.81:1.1: cck17.84, csp1ed13.78, csp2ed13.78, ms13S3_434, msdiv81, pts-vp-pli3.95, sya17.115
